mnemonics: Collections are like a library. You can't just shove a book (your object) on a shelf. It needs a unique catalog number (hashCode) and must be organized so others can find it (equals).
keyPoints: You MUST override the hashCode() method.
You MUST override the equals(Object obj) method.
For sorted collections like TreeSet, your class must implement the Comparable interface or be provided with a Comparator.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to override hashCode() when you override equals(), which breaks the general contract for hashCode.
Using == instead of the equals() method to compare object contents inside your overridden equals method.
Not making the hashCode() implementation consistent with equals() (i.e., two objects that are equal must have the same hash code).
